prs.title: feat: sales context engine — objections, visit strategy, memory, humanization
prs.body:
## Summary

Implements Phases 3-8 of the next-gen conversation architecture, transforming the bot from a Q&A database into a professional real-estate sales consultant.

### New modules (`src/modules/sales/`)

- **PropertyAdvantages.ts** — Derives approved sales insights from PropertyFacts (connectivity, community, developer trust, inventory, value, timeline)
- **CommonObjections.ts** — Deterministic playbooks for 7 objection types: expensive, not interested, exploring others, family approval, need info, busy now, call later. Pattern: Acknowledge → Value → Soft guide. All bypass LLM.
- **VisitPitches.ts** — Interest-score-gated visit invitation templates. Only triggered after ≥3 unique property questions, max 1 invite per call.
- **ConversationMemory.ts** — Per-call memory tracking: questions asked, interest score, objections raised, visit invites shown, last intent/topic. Prevents repeated answers, repeated objections, repeated visit asks.
- **Humanizer.ts** — Controlled acknowledgements (Ji/Bilkul/Samajh gaya/Haan/Achha) on ~5-10% of responses with 5-turn cooldown. Never on questions, never on first turn.

### Routing changes (`CallOrchestrator.ts`)

```
User utterance
  → classifyIntent()                    (sync, <1ms)
  → Property query?  → deterministic response + maybe visit invite
  → Objection?       → deterministic playbook
  → Visit rejection? → deterministic response
  → Unknown?         → Groq LLM (only fallback)
```

### New metrics

- `OBJECTION_PLAYBOOK_USED` — count of objections handled without LLM
- `VISIT_INVITE_TRIGGERED` — count of interest-gated visit invitations
- `ConversationMemorySummary` — logged at call end with full memory state

## Test plan

- [x] 195 unit tests pass (46 new sales engine + 38 intent routing + 25 enrichment + 86 barge-in)
- [x] TypeScript compiles cleanly
- [ ] Live call: ask price, location, amenities → verify deterministic responses
- [ ] Live call: say "mehnga hai" → verify objection playbook response (no LLM)
- [ ] Live call: ask 3+ property questions → verify visit invite appears
- [ ] Live call: verify visit invite never repeats
- [ ] Check `IntentRoutingSummary` and `ConversationMemorySummary` in logs
- [ ] Verify no regression in scheduling flow or barge-in behavior

prs.title: feat: consultative response enrichment + intent-based LLM bypass
prs.body:
## Summary

- **Response Enrichment Layer**: Injects approved contextual insights into LLM prompts so responses sound like a consultant, not a database (e.g. "8 to 10 thousand per sqft hai. Competitive pricing segment mein aata hai.")
- **Intent-Based LLM Bypass**: Regex-based classifier routes property queries (PRICE, LOCATION, AMENITIES, BHK, POSSESSION, DEVELOPER, UNITS) to deterministic response builders — skips Groq LLM entirely, saving ~150ms per query
- **Sarvam Pipeline Integration**: Locally-routed responses go directly to SarvamTTS via `speakCanned()`, same path as scheduling templates
- **Metrics**: Per-call tracking of local vs LLM routing, classification time (<1ms), intent distribution, estimated latency savings

prs.title: feat: deterministic state-driven conversation architecture
prs.body:
## Summary

- **State machine**: `GET_NAME → ANSWERING → ASK_DAY → ASK_TIME → CONFIRMING → BOOKED` — app drives conversation, not the LLM
- **Entity extraction**: regex-based (sync, zero latency) + Groq llama-3.1-8b-instant fallback for ambiguous utterances
- **Context injection**: `[PROPERTY FACTS]` + `[SESSION STATE]` + `[NEXT ACTION]` injected before every Nova response
- **Response validation**: blocks hallucinated bookings, invented prices/dates/amenities
- **System prompt**: shrunk from ~800 to ~194 tokens — conversation flow removed (state machine handles it)
- **Cleanup**: removed `apps/web`, `apps/api`, `apps/voice-agent-nova-elevenlabs`, `packages/*`, `turbo.json`, turbo scripts

## New files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/modules/conversation/ConversationTypes.ts` | SessionState, ConversationStep, PropertyFacts, ExtractionResult, ValidationIssue |
| `src/modules/conversation/PropertyFacts.ts` | Static Akshay Vista property data |
| `src/modules/conversation/EntityExtractor.ts` | Regex extraction + Groq fallback |
| `src/modules/conversation/ConversationStateManager.ts` | Per-session state machine |
| `src/modules/conversation/PromptBuilder.ts` | Builds context block for injection |
| `src/modules/conversation/ResponseValidator.ts` | Post-hoc response validation |

## Test plan

- [x] `npm run type-check` passes
- [x] `npm run build` passes
- [x] `npm test` — 86/86 tests pass
- [ ] Live call test: verify name capture on first turn
- [ ] Live call test: verify booking requires both day + time
- [ ] Live call test: verify no invented amenities in responses

prs.title: feat(orm): rename reputation to orm module and add gbp reviews tab
prs.body:
## Summary

- Renames the entire Reputation module → ORM (Online Reputation Management) across backend and frontend, preserving all existing DB table names (`@@map`) so no data migration is needed
- Adds 8-dimension AI-powered ORM scoring via Groq (`llama-3.3-70b-versatile`) across search presence, news coverage, review health, online visibility, sentiment, competitive position, content authority, and crisis risk

  - Google Business Profile OAuth connect flow (only prompted when clicking Reply)

- Tab state managed via URL param (`?tab=overview|reviews`) — shallow routing, no full reload
- New Prisma models: `GbpLocation`, `GbpReplyHistory`; `gbpCredentials Json?` (AES-256-GCM encrypted) added to `OrmConfig`

prs.title: feat: brand audit auth stripping and theme unification
prs.body:
## Summary

- **ASM side**: `growth-brand-audits.page.tsx` now sends `postMessage({type:'ASM_AUTH', user})` to the Brand Audit iframe immediately after load and whenever ASM auth state changes; sends `ASM_LOGOUT` on signout
- **Brand Audit side** (separate PR: shibaditya-propacity/Brand-audit): deleted `middleware.ts`, rewrote `AuthProvider` to listen for postMessage in embedded mode, replaced all `indigo-` brand colors with `primary` (#2D3FE7), hidden TopBar + correct background in embedded mode

## How it works

1. User clicks Brand Audits in ASM sidebar → iframe loads at `BRAND_AUDIT_URL?embedded=1`
2. After load, ASM sends `{ type: 'ASM_AUTH', user: { name, email } }` via postMessage
3. Brand Audit's `AuthProvider` receives this and sets user — no cookie needed
4. If no message arrives within 2s (e.g. standalone tab), falls back to cookie auth
5. On ASM logout, `ASM_LOGOUT` message is sent to clear Brand Audit session

prs.title: feat: integrate Brand Audit app into growth engine
prs.body:
## Summary

Integrates the standalone Brand Audit Next.js app (`Brand-audit/`) into the Growth Engine's `/growth/brand-audits` route.

### Integration strategy chosen: Option B — iframe embed

The Brand Audit app cannot be copied as React components (Option A) because it has:
- **34 Next.js API routes** connecting to MongoDB, Claude AI, and SSE streaming
- **8 external APIs** — PDL, Serper, WebCrawler, HikerAPI, Meta Ad Library, Apify, Microlink, YouTube
- **MongoDB** data models (separate from Growth Engine's PostgreSQL)
- Its own auth system, wizard flow, 10 dimension analysis pages, print-to-PDF reports

Migrating all of this to Vite/Express would be a multi-month rewrite with high risk.

### What changed

**Growth Engine (`apps/frontend/`):**
- `growth-brand-audits.page.tsx` — rewritten as a full-height iframe shell with:
  - Animated skeleton while iframe loads
  - "Not configured" state if `VITE_BRAND_AUDIT_URL` is unset (with setup instructions)
  - Error state if the Brand Audit service is unreachable
  - "Open in tab" link in the Growth Engine topbar
  - Appends `?embedded=1` to hide Brand Audit's own navigation
- `apps/frontend/.env.example` — documents `VITE_BRAND_AUDIT_URL`

**Brand Audit app (`Brand-audit/` — sibling folder):**
- `globals.css` — primary color updated from indigo (`#6366f1`) to brand blue (`#2D3FE7`), pulse-ring + gradient-text updated to match
- `tailwind.config.ts` — `font-sans: DM Sans` and `font-heading: Sora` added (matches Growth Engine's font stack)
- `src/app/layout.tsx` — DM Sans + Sora loaded via `next/font/google`; `EmbeddedModeDetector` added
- New `src/components/shared/EmbeddedModeDetector.tsx` — detects `?embedded=1`, adds `.embedded` class to body; `globals.css` then hides the sticky `<header>` so Growth Engine's topbar is the only navigation chrome visible
- `package.json` — dev port changed from 3000 → 3001 (avoids conflict with Growth Engine backend)
- `.env.example` — created with all 15 required/optional env vars documented

### How to run locally

```bash
# Terminal 1 — Growth Engine backend
cd propacity-ASM && pnpm --filter @asm/backend dev

# Terminal 2 — Growth Engine frontend
cd propacity-ASM && pnpm --filter @asm/frontend dev

# Terminal 3 — Brand Audit (new)
cd Brand-audit && npm run dev    # runs on :3001

# Then add to apps/frontend/.env.local:
# VITE_BRAND_AUDIT_URL=http://localhost:3001
```

### Sidebar routing
The "Brand Audits" nav item already exists in the sidebar (`app-shell.tsx`) pointing to `/growth/brand-audits`. No sidebar changes needed — active state highlighting works correctly.

prs.title: Add cursor-pointer to "Learn more" button
prs.body:
# Summary
This pull request improves the user experience on the landing page by updating the **"Learn more"** button to display a pointer cursor on hover. This aligns the interaction behavior with the **"Try it for yourself"** button and provides a consistent visual cue indicating that the element is clickable.

---

# What Was Fixed
- The "Learn more" button previously displayed the default arrow cursor on hover.
- This created an inconsistent experience compared to the primary CTA.
- Added the `cursor-pointer` utility to clearly indicate interactivity.

---

# Changes Made
Updated the button class in `src/pages/LandingPage.jsx`.

## Before

<button
  className="py-3 mb-4 xl:mb-0 mr-4 transition-all duration-300 bg-white border rounded-full shadow-lg px-9 border-zinc-200 hover:bg-zinc-100"
  onClick={() =>
    document
      .getElementById("learn-more")
      .scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth" })
  }
>
  Learn more
</button>

## After

<button
  className="py-3 mb-4 xl:mb-0 mr-4 transition-all duration-300 bg-white border rounded-full shadow-lg px-9 border-zinc-200 hover:bg-zinc-100 cursor-pointer"
  onClick={() =>
    document
      .getElementById("learn-more")
      .scrollIntoView({ behavior: "smooth" })
  }
>
  Learn more
</button>

#Before

- Cursor remained as a default arrow
- Button appeared clickable but didn’t communicate it visually
- Inconsistent with “Try it for yourself” button

#After

- Pointer cursor shown on hover
- Clear indication that the button is interactive
- Consistent UX with other CTAs
- Improves visual feedback and accessibility

#Testing Performed

-  Pointer cursor appears correctly on hover
-  All existing styles remain unchanged
-  Smooth scroll behavior unaffected
-  No layout or visual regressions


#Files Changed
src/pages/LandingPage.jsx
Added cursor-pointer to the "Learn more" button class list

prs.title: Feature: Add Hackathon Listing Page with Devpost API
prs.body: ### What’s changed / added - Added a new page **`/hackathons`** implemented in `HackathonsPage.jsx`, which fetches upcoming hackathons from **Devpost API** (`https://devpost.com/api/hackathons?challenge_type=all&status=upcoming`) and renders them in a card-grid UI. - Created presentation & layout components: - `HackathonGrid.jsx` — handles mapping over events, pagination / “Load more” logic. - `HackathonCard.jsx` — displays each hackathon’s thumbnail, title, dates, location, participation mode (Online/Offline/Hybrid), and a **“Participate”** button linking to the official event URL. - Updated existing navbar (e.g. in `Navbar.jsx`) — added a new nav link **“Upcoming hackathons”** that leads to `/hackathons`. - Added robust error handling & UX: - Loading indicator while fetch is ongoing. - Friendly error message + retry option if fetch fails (network, CORS, parsing, etc.). - Graceful fallback for missing data (no thumbnail, missing dates, missing URL, missing location, etc.). - URL normalization — Devpost’s `thumbnail_url` often begins with `//`; code converts to full `https://...` before rendering. - Parsed the API response expecting JSON of shape: ```json { "hackathons": [ /* list of events */ ] } ``` Then internal code uses `json.hackathons || []` to avoid runtime crashes if structure is different. - Maintained UI consistency — did **not** change existing global styles or CSS variables; new components follow the styling patterns used across the project. - Client-side only. No external libraries introduced — uses only React + JS + CSS (or existing styling framework in project). ### Devpost API used ``` https://devpost.com/api/hackathons?challenge_type=all&status=upcoming ``` - Returns JSON containing upcoming/ongoing hackathons globally. - We fetch this endpoint on page load when user navigates to `/hackathons`. - Example JSON structure (simplified): ```json { "hackathons": [ { "id": 27230, "title": "SAP Business AI Innovation Challenge", "thumbnail_url": "//d112y698adiu2z.cloudfront.net/…/medium_square.png", "displayed_location": { "icon": "globe", "location": "Online" }, "submission_period_dates": "Dec 01, 2025 - Jan 07, 2026", "url": "https://sap.devpost.com/", … }, … ] } ``` ### How to test locally 1. Run frontend dev server: ```bash npm run dev ``` 2. Open browser at: ``` http://localhost:3000/hackathons ``` 3. Observe: - A loading indicator while fetch happens. - After load — a grid of hackathon cards. - Each card should show thumbnail, title, dates, location, mode (Online/Offline), and a “Participate” button. - Clicking “Participate” opens the event’s site in a new tab. - If any data is missing (e.g. no thumbnail), card still renders gracefully with placeholder/fallback. - Simulate a fetch failure (e.g. by disabling network) — page should show error message + retry button. Clicking retry should attempt fetch again. - Responsive layout: cards adjust for mobile/tablet/desktop sizes. ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **New Features** * Added dedicated Hackathons page with search functionality, type and location filtering, and pagination support. * New "Upcoming hackathons" navigation link. * Introduced toast notification and dialog systems. * **Style** * Added fade-up and float animations for enhanced visual effects. ✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.

prs.title: Add copy-to-clipboard buttons for code blocks in /docs routes
prs.body:
Adds interactive copy buttons to all code blocks displayed in the /docs routes and child pages. The feature enhances user experience by allowing readers to quickly copy code snippets with a single click. ## Features **Button Behavior:** - Compact chip-style button positioned at top-right corner of each code block - Works on all elements with `ch-code*` or `ch-codeblock*` classes, with fallback to `
`
- One button per code block with duplicate prevention via dataset tracking

**User Interactions:**
- Click to copy code to clipboard
- Keyboard support: Enter or Space keys to activate
- Visual feedback: Button text changes to "Copied!" (green) for 1.5 seconds on success
- Error state displays "Failed" (red) if copy operation fails
- Smooth hover effects with opacity and shadow transitions

**Technical Implementation:**
- Inline styles with `!important` flags to override inherited page CSS
- Responsive positioning: uses parent element with relative positioning
- Preserves line breaks using `innerText` extraction
- Modern Clipboard API with fallback to `execCommand('copy')` for older browsers
- MutationObserver watches for dynamically loaded code blocks
- Accessibility: full ARIA labels and keyboard support
- No global CSS files modified—styles applied directly to elements

**Scope:**
- Only activates on routes starting with `/docs`
- Does not modify existing code, styles, or documentation
- Non-intrusive enhancement that preserves all existing functionality

## Testing
Verified on multiple code block types with various content lengths. Button displays correctly in all states and integrates seamlessly with existing documentation layout.

prs.title: Added copy code button functionality in code blocks
prs.body:
**Summary** This PR adds a "Copy to Clipboard" button feature to all code blocks (`
` tags) throughout the `/concepts` section and all its child routes on the webpack.js.org documentation site. The implementation enhances user experience by allowing visitors to quickly copy code examples to their clipboard with a single click.

**Motivation:**
- Users frequently need to copy code examples from documentation
- Manual text selection and copying is cumbersome and error-prone
- A dedicated copy button provides a seamless, one-click experience
- The feature is non-intrusive and doesn't interfere with existing documentation styling

**Related Issues:**
- Improves documentation usability
- Follows modern documentation UI patterns used by sites like MDN, Vercel, and others



**What kind of change does this PR introduce?**

This PR introduces a **feature enhancement** that adds interactive UI elements to documentation pages without modifying any content files or breaking changes.

**Changes Include:**
-  New `CopyCodeButton` React component with clipboard functionality
-  Integration into the `Page` component for automatic code block enhancement
-  SCSS styling with light/dark mode support
-  Proper React 17 compatibility (using `ReactDOM.render`)
-  Error handling and user feedback ("✓ Copied!" notification)

**Did you add tests for your changes?**

Yes, comprehensive tests have been added:
-  5/5 unit tests passing
-  Component renders correctly
-  Copy button displays proper text
-  Clipboard functionality verified
-  Feedback state management tested
-  Text revert after timeout verified
-  ESLint validation: 0 errors

**Test Results:**
```
PASS  src/components/CopyCodeButton/CopyCodeButton.test.jsx
  CopyCodeButton
    ✓ renders the button (XX ms)
    ✓ displays Copy text by default (X ms)
    ✓ copies text to clipboard when clicked (XX ms)
    ✓ shows Copied feedback (X ms)
    ✓ reverts text after 2 seconds (XX ms)

Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       5 passed, 5 total

```

**Does this PR introduce a breaking change?**

No, this PR introduces **zero breaking changes**:
-  No changes to existing component APIs
-  No modifications to documentation content files
-  No alterations to routing or navigation
-  Dynamic injection only - no impact on server-side rendering
-  Fully backward compatible with React 17 and React Router 6
-  Graceful fallback if clipboard API unavailable

**If relevant, what needs to be documented once your changes are merged or what have you already documented?**

No additional documentation needed:
-  Feature is self-explanatory (visual "Copy" button)
-  No user-facing configuration required
-  Works automatically on all `/concepts/*` pages
-  No API changes or public exports to document
-  Implementation is internal and transparent to documentation authors

---

## Implementation Details

### Files Added:
1. **`src/components/CopyCodeButton/CopyCodeButton.jsx`** (33 lines)
   - React component with copy-to-clipboard functionality
   - Displays "Copy" normally, "✓ Copied!" on click
   - 2-second feedback duration
   - Error handling for clipboard API failures
   - PropTypes validation

2. **`src/components/CopyCodeButton/CopyCodeButton.scss`** (45 lines)
   - Absolute positioning styling
   - Hover and focus states
   - Light/dark mode support
   - Responsive design

### Files Modified:
1. **`src/components/Page/Page.jsx`**
   - Added `import CopyCodeButton from '../CopyCodeButton/CopyCodeButton'`
   - Added `import ReactDOM from 'react-dom'`
   - Added `useEffect` hook to enhance code blocks when content loads
   - Targets only `/concepts` routes
   - Runs enhancement at page load and after delays to catch async content

### How It Works:
1. When a page in `/concepts/*` loads, the `Page` component detects content has loaded
2. Enhancement function queries all `
` elements in the page
3. For each code block, creates an absolutely-positioned wrapper
4. Renders the `CopyCodeButton` component into the wrapper
5. Button is placed at `top: 8px, right: 8px` with `z-index: 10`
6. Tracks enhanced blocks via `dataset.copyButtonAdded` flag to avoid duplicates
7. Runs retry logic at 300ms and 1000ms to catch dynamically-loaded content

### Browser Compatibility:
-  Uses modern Clipboard API (`navigator.clipboard.writeText`)
-  Tested on macOS
-  Fallback error handling for unsupported browsers


### Performance:
-  No impact on bundle size (minimal component)
-  Only activates on `/concepts` routes
-  Efficient DOM querying (single `querySelectorAll` call)
-  Memory managed via proper cleanup

---

## Visual Representation

### Light Mode:
```
┌─────────────────────────────────┐
│ webpack.config.js       [Copy]◄─┤ Button appears top-right
├─────────────────────────────────┤

│ };                              │
```

```
│ };                              │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

### After Click:

```
├─────────────────────────────────┤
│ module.exports = {              │
│   entry: './src/index.js',      │
│ };                              │
└─────────────────────────────────┘
```

---

## Testing Evidence

### Code Quality:
-  ESLint: 0 errors, 0 warnings
-  PropTypes validation: Complete
-  React 17 compatibility: Verified
-  No console errors on `/concepts` pages

### Manual Testing:

No special deployment steps required:

-  No environment variables needed
-  No database changes
-  No server-side changes
-  Can be deployed with regular CI/CD pipeline

issues.title: Fix Needed: Copilot Chat Fails Due to Deprecated API Version
issues.body:
- Copilot Chat Extension Version: 0.32 (2025-10-08)
- VS Code Version: 1.109.0
- OS Version: macOS 10.15.7 (Intel)
- Feature (e.g. agent/edit/ask mode): Copilot Chat (Ask Mode / Inline Chat)
- Selected model (e.g. GPT 4.1, Claude 3.7 Sonnet): Default (Auto-selected by Copilot)

- Logs:
Sorry, your request failed. Please try again.

Copilot Request id: ced7cfd8-8295-4cfa-82bf-a9ac5b1d4dcb

GH Request Id: C783:65081:4D952C1:57E091E:698C2C93

Reason: client not supported: bad request: the specified API version is no longer supported.
You may need to update your client to a newer version.


Steps to Reproduce:

1. Open VS Code (v1.109.0) on macOS.
2. Open any workspace or file.
3. Open GitHub Copilot Chat.
4. Enter any prompt in Ask / Chat mode.
5. Send the request.

Expected Result:

Copilot should respond normally to chat prompts.

Actual Result:

Copilot fails with an error stating that the client API version is no longer supported.

Additional Notes:

- VS Code is fully up to date.
- Copilot Chat extension appears outdated and does not auto-update.
- Reinstalling the extension and re-authenticating GitHub does not resolve the issue.
- The error persists after restarting VS Code and clearing cache.

issues.title: Feature Request: Dark and Light Theme Toggle
issues.body:
* [x] I agree to follow the [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/jgraph/drawio/blob/dev/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md) that this project adheres to.
* [x] I have searched the issue tracker for a feature request that matches the one I want to file, without success.

**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, the application does not provide a built-in option to toggle between light and dark themes. Users working for long durations, especially in low-light environments, may experience eye strain. Additionally, users who prefer dark mode for accessibility or consistency with their system settings cannot align the interface with their preference.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Introduce a Dark / Light Mode toggle that allows users to manually switch between themes. The toggle could be placed in the toolbar or settings menu, support system-level theme detection (prefers-color-scheme), and persist the selected theme across sessions using local storage.

**Describe alternatives you've considered**
Using browser extensions or OS-level forced dark mode, which often results in inconsistent UI behavior and visual glitches. Another alternative is manually adjusting background and element colors, which is not practical or user-friendly.

**Additional context**
Adding a Dark / Light Mode toggle would improve accessibility, reduce eye strain, and align the application with modern UI/UX standards followed by most contemporary productivity tools.

issues.title: Feature Request: Add “Copy SVG” Option Alongside Download for Icons
issues.body:
**Description**
Currently, Feather Icons provides a Download option for SVG icons, but there is no direct Copy SVG option. This makes quick usage in development workflows slightly inefficient, especially when developers want to paste SVGs directly into codebases (HTML, JSX, Vue, etc.).

**Problem**
Downloading SVG files for every icon adds extra steps:

Download file

Open file

Copy SVG markup

Paste into project

For rapid UI development, this slows down workflows and breaks consistency compared to other icon libraries.

**Proposed Solution**
Add a “Copy SVG” button next to the existing Download option for every icon, allowing users to:

Copy the raw SVG markup to clipboard in one click

Use icons instantly without downloading files

Maintain a standardized, developer-friendly workflow

**Benefits**

Faster development experience

Improved usability for frontend developers

Aligns Feather Icons with modern design-system tools

Keeps SVG usage consistent across frameworks

**Optional Enhancements**

Toast notification: “SVG copied to clipboard”

Option to copy optimized/minified SVG

Keyboard shortcut support

**Expected Behavior**

Users should be able to both Copy and Download SVG icons

Feature should be consistent across all icons

issues.title: [FEATURE] :a missing Dark/White Mode toggle feature.
issues.body:
**Is your feature request related to a problem? Please describe.**
Currently, the application does not include a Dark/Light mode toggle in the Landing Page, which limits user personalization and can cause discomfort when using the app in low-light or high-light environments.
Users who prefer dark mode for accessibility, eye strain reduction, or night-time use cannot switch themes.

**Describe the solution you'd like**
Add a Dark/Light mode toggle to the UI—preferably in the header, sidebar, or settings menu.

The toggle should:
Instantly switch between dark and light themes
Remember user preference (localStorage or system preference)
Apply theme consistently across all pages and components
This feature would greatly enhance usability and user comfort.

**Additional context**
A theme toggle is a standard modern UI feature and would make the app more accessible and user-friendly.

issues.title: [BUG]:Database Selection Options Not Translated to Bengali
issues.body: **Describe the bug** When Bengali (বাংলা) is selected as the language, most UI elements are correctly translated. However, on the database selection screen, all database names (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite, MariaDB, MSSQL, Oracle SQL, Generic) still appear in English instead of Bengali. This creates inconsistency in localization. **To Reproduce** - Steps to reproduce the behavior: - Go to Settings → Language - Select বাংলা (Bengali) - Navigate to the Database Selection screen - Observe that all database option titles are still in English **Expected behavior** All database names and labels on the database selection screen should appear in Bengali, consistent with the rest of the UI translation. **Screenshots** **Desktop:** OS: macOS Browser: Chrome **Additional context** The descriptive Bengali text below the database options appears correctly translated, but the database names themselves seem to be hardcoded or missing localization keys. Please check Bengali locale JSON and ensure all database labels use the translation function.

issues.title: Feature Request: Add "Copy" Button to Code Blocks in Documentation
Feature: Add “Copy” button to all code blocks (pre > code) in docs (persisted UX & accessible)
issues.body:
**Summary** Add a small “Copy” button to every code block (
…
) across documentation pages (including installation guides). The button copies the code to clipboard, gives immediate visual feedback (e.g. “Copied!”), is keyboard accessible, and works for dynamically rendered content (client-side navigation). Use progressive enhancement (works if JavaScript is enabled; if not, code remains readable). **Why** Improves developer DX: copying long commands / snippets is tedious without a one-click button. Standard expectation for docs & reduces copy/paste errors. Accessibility-friendly copy affordance helps keyboard & screen-reader users. **Proposed solution** Add a small Vue 2 client-side enhancement that: Finds all 
 blocks and inserts a copy button.

Uses the modern Clipboard API with a robust fallback.

Shows visual feedback (tooltip text changes to “Copied!” then reverts).

Supports keyboard activation and has appropriate aria-* attributes.

Handles dynamically inserted code blocks (MutationObserver).

Minimal CSS to match docs styling.

Add a single small global CSS file for the copy button.

Add tests/manual checklist.

issues.title: Feature request: Missing Dark/Light Mode Toggle Support
issues.body:
### Is your feature request related to a problem?

_No response_

### Feature description

I would like to request the addition of a **Dark/Light Mode Toggle** to improve accessibility and user experience. The feature can be implemented using a simple theme state stored in localStorage and applied via CSS variables.

At a high level, the implementation would include:

**Theme Context / State**
A global theme state (light/dark) that updates the UI and persists between sessions.

**Toggle Button**
A small UI button (e.g., in the header) that switches between dark and light themes with a single click.

**CSS Variable Themes**
Use data-theme="light" and data-theme="dark" on the <html> or <body> tag to swap theme-specific color variables.

Persistence
Store the selected theme in localStorage so the user’s preference is retained on reload.

System Preference Support (optional)
Automatically detect prefers-color-scheme to set an initial default theme.

This feature would modernize the UI, improve accessibility, and align the project with standard UX expectations.

### Alternatives

_No response_

### Additional context

If the maintainers are open to it, I would be happy to contribute by implementing this feature.

### Feature request checklist

- [x] The title starts with "Feature request: " and is followed by the requested feature description
- [x] This is a single feature
- [x] I have [searched for existing issues](https://github.com/FortAwesome/Font-Awesome/issues) and to the best of my knowledge this is not a duplicate

issues.title: Enhancement: Dark Mode Toggle & “Copy Code” Button Missing in Documentation Pages
issues.body:
### 📌 Feature Request: Dark Mode + Copy Code Button on Documentation Site

**Website:** https://lodash.com/  
**Repository:** https://github.com/lodash/lodash

---

### Problem

Currently, the documentation on https://lodash.com/ lacks:

1. **Dark mode toggle**
   - Reading the docs for long periods can strain the eyes, especially in low-light environments.
   - The website has a dark-themed code example section, but the rest of the UI stays bright, making the contrast uncomfortable.

2. **"Copy" button for code snippets**
   - While exploring the docs, users need to manually select code to copy.
   - A single-click "Copy Code" button (like many modern documentation sites provide) would improve developer experience.

---

### 💡 Proposed Solution

- Add a **dark/light mode toggle** (persist user preference in localStorage).
- Add a **copy-to-clipboard button** to code examples using `navigator.clipboard.writeText()`.

---

### ✅ Benefits

| Feature | Benefit |
|---------|---------|
| Dark mode toggle | Better readability, accessibility, and modern UI experience. |
| Copy code button | Faster workflow, improves DX, avoids manual text selection errors. |

---


### 🙌 Thank you

Lodash is an amazing library used by millions. These small UI improvements will significantly enhance the documentation experience for developers.
